question_title: What will be the value of "Crude Oil Prices: Brent - Europe" on 2026-02-13?
summary: Brent crude oil (FRED DCOILBRENTEU) was $67.72 on Feb 2, 2026, after a sharp 6.3% single-day drop from $72.25 (likely tariff-related). The resolution date is Feb 13, just 9 trading days away. I ran a Monte Carlo simulation using 6 months of empirical daily volatility (~1.8% full-sample, ~2.6% recent), blending neutral-drift, high-vol, and slight-negative-drift scenarios. The distribution centers near $67.5 (current price) with wide tails reflecting elevated recent volatility. The bearish medium-term outlook (EIA: $56/b avg for 2026) creates slight downside bias but has limited impact over 9 days. Key risk factors: tariff escalation could push prices lower; tariff de-escalation or OPEC+ response could push prices higher.

factors.description: Current price anchor at $67.72 on Feb 2 - 9 trading days to resolution anchors the distribution
Sharp 6.3% single-day drop on Feb 2 (tariff shock) suggests elevated volatility and possible further downside
EIA forecasts Brent averaging $56/b in 2026, ABN AMRO $55/b - bearish medium-term fundamentals
Jan 2026 rally from $60 to $72 partially reversed - mean reversion tendency
Tariff-shock selloffs can quickly reverse if tensions ease
Monte Carlo simulation (100k paths, blended vol scenarios) produces well-calibrated distribution centered at ~$67.5

reasoning:
**Brent Crude Oil Price Forecast for Feb 13, 2026**

Starting from $67.72 on Feb 2 (after a 6.3% tariff-shock drop), the 9-day forecast is centered near the current price with meaningful uncertainty due to elevated volatility:

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| 10th | $62.40 |
| 20th | $64.10 |
| 40th | $66.40 |
| 60th | $68.50 |
| 80th | $71.00 |
| 90th | $73.10 |

**Key drivers:**
- The distribution anchors on the current $67.72 price — over 9 days, oil is essentially a random walk
- Elevated recent volatility (~2.6% daily vs 1.8% long-run) widens the tails
- Slight downside skew from bearish fundamentals (EIA/ABN AMRO forecasting $55-56 avg for 2026) and tariff uncertainty
- Upside possible if tariff tensions ease or OPEC+ signals support
